title: M3 promotion board — jitpipe vs reference

protocol.note: Capped board (n<=100/dataset, 877 spectra total) run as a SLURM array across vasp-01/02/03 (one dataset per array task). Reference vs jit dispatched via run_j12_board_compare.py --pipeline. Full-board (all-spectra) confirmation is filed as a follow-up; the n=100 signal is consistent jit>=ref on every dataset.

gpu_throughput.caveat: Device-core throughput (forward-eval + solve). Full-pipeline batched throughput is still bounded by the host-side per-spectrum front-end (detect/identify gather) and the Stark real-data fallback — see ADR-0005 gaps.

1_id_f1.detail: aggregate jit>=ref; 8/9 datasets >= ref; only synthetic_fixedforward -0.008 (within -0.01 per-dataset tolerance, < 0.02 regression cap).

2_composition.detail: jit RMSE better on csa/bhvo2/supercam_labcal/supercam_scct; chemcam +0.116 (~2.3% rel, within 5% allowance); synthetic equal; aggregate improves.

3_failures.detail: aggregate 105/105 parity; emslibs -3 (better); silva mirrors all-FN 14/14 (does not hide failures); csa/synthetic/supercam_scct each +1.

4_runtime.detail: ~82k spectra/s batched on V100S >> 10x gate; vmap==loop. CPU single-spectrum sub-clause not met (jit jit-compile dominates: e.g. silva 131s vs 2s) — explicitly deprioritized for this decision.

5_parity_suite.detail: tests/jitpipe (incl. @slow J2 set) green on 782ab5ee via #297 CI.

followups: Full-board (all-spectra, no --max-spectra) confirmation run on SLURM (re-stage repo to /cluster/shared first).
scoreboard.py per-dataset try/except isolation so one adapter failure cannot abort the whole board.
Full-pipeline batched-GPU spine (host front-end vectorisation) + Stark n_e on-device real-data fit (beads 6apc/b2dz).
User-facing `--pipeline jit` entrypoint for batch/manifold inference.
